agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and entity-to-entity money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), tiered account verification, and fraud prevention controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.",
    "bounded_context": "Wallet Transfers & Account Compliance",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "KYC (Know Your Customer)", "definition": "Identity verification process required by financial regulations before a user is granted full transactional capabilities." },
      { "term": "Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed full KYC identity verification. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000." },
      { "term": "Partially-Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Can send transfers up to a $500 daily limit. Can receive funds. Cannot withdraw." },
      { "term": "Unverified Account", "definition": "An account that has not begun or completed any KYC verification. Can receive money only. Cannot send or withdraw." },
      { "term": "Settlement", "definition": "The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation). Once settled, the transaction is irreversible." },
      { "term": "Sanctioned Entity", "definition": "An individual, organization, or country appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC, EU sanctions). Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked before execution." },
      { "term": "Account Lock", "definition": "A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. A locked account cannot be accessed until a support agent manually unlocks it." },
      { "term": "Daily Transfer Limit", "definition": "The maximum cumulative outbound transfer amount a user may initiate within a rolling 24-hour calendar day, determined by verification tier." },
      { "term": "Transaction Notification", "definition": "An alert sent to the account holder for any single transaction exceeding $1,000 in value." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "An unverified account CANNOT initiate a send or withdrawal transaction. Only receive is permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Allowing unverified users to move money out of the system violates KYC/AML regulatory requirements. This is a legal compliance constraint, not a policy preference — violation could result in regulatory action.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to send or withdraw from an account with verification_status = 'unverified' must be rejected with an appropriate error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $5,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $5,000 threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted by product or compliance leadership without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "The sum of all outbound transfers for a verified account within a calendar day must not exceed $5,000. Any transfer that would cause the cumulative total to exceed $5,000 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Partially-verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $500.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $500 threshold is a business policy tunable by product or compliance leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "The sum of all outbound transfers for a partially-verified account within a calendar day must not exceed $500. Any transfer that would cause the cumulative total to exceed $500 must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "A transaction CANNOT be reversed once it has reached settled status (T+1).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Settlement finality is a core financial system integrity constraint. Reversing a settled transaction would corrupt the ledger and break double-entry accounting invariants. Disputes on settled transactions must go through a separate refund/chargeback process, not a reversal.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any reversal request against a transaction with status = 'settled' must be rejected. Only transactions with status = 'pending' (pre-settlement) may be reversed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Transfers to sanctioned entities MUST be blocked automatically before execution.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Sanctions screening is a legal obligation under AML regulations. Allowing a transfer to a sanctioned entity, even momentarily, constitutes a violation. This is not a deferrable policy — it is a hard compliance gate.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every outbound transfer must be screened against the active sanctions list before funds move. If the recipient matches a sanctioned entity, the transfer must be blocked, the transaction must be flagged, and the funds must not leave the sender's account."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Five consecutive failed login attempts MUST lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock it.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Account lock on repeated failure is a security integrity constraint. If the system fails to lock, brute-force attacks succeed. The manual-unlock-only requirement prevents automated bypass of the security control.",
      "testable_assertion": "After 5 consecutive failed login attempts on the same account, the account status must transition to 'locked'. A locked account must reject all login attempts regardless of correct credentials. Only an authenticated support agent action can transition the account back to 'active'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "A notification must be sent for any single transaction exceeding $1,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $1,000 notification threshold is a product/UX policy. Failure to notify is a degraded experience, not data corruption. The threshold and notification channel could be changed by product leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is initiated (send or receive), a notification event must be emitted targeting the account holder."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Unverified and partially-verified accounts CANNOT withdraw funds.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Withdrawals move money out of the regulated system. Permitting withdrawal without full verification violates KYC requirements for off-ramp transactions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any withdrawal attempt from an account with verification_status != 'verified' must be rejected."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "An unverified account with a $200 balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $50 to another account",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required, and the balance remains $200"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "An unverified account",
      "when": "Another user sends $300 to this account",
      "then": "The transfer succeeds and the recipient's balance increases by $300"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A verified account that has already sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $250",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit, and the balance is unchanged"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A partially-verified account that has sent $0 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $501",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A partially-verified account that has sent $400 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $100",
      "then": "The transaction succeeds because the cumulative total ($500) does not exceed the daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A transaction initiated yesterday that has reached settled status (T+1)",
      "when": "A reversal is requested on that transaction",
      "then": "The reversal is rejected with an error indicating settled transactions cannot be reversed"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A transaction initiated today that is still in pending status",
      "when": "A reversal is requested on that transaction",
      "then": "The reversal succeeds and the funds are returned to the sender"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A verified account attempting to send $2,000 to a recipient on the sanctions list",
      "when": "The transfer is submitted",
      "then": "The transfer is blocked before execution, the sender's balance is unchanged, and the transaction is flagged for compliance review"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A user account with 4 consecutive failed login attempts",
      "when": "The user fails a 5th consecutive login attempt",
      "then": "The account is locked immediately and all subsequent login attempts are rejected, even with correct credentials"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "The user enters correct credentials",
      "then": "Login is rejected and the account remains locked"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "A support agent initiates an unlock action",
      "then": "The account is unlocked and the failed login counter is reset to zero"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A verified account sending $1,500 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is processed successfully",
      "then": "A notification is sent to the account holder indicating the transaction details"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A verified account sending $999 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is processed successfully",
      "then": "No high-value transaction notification is triggered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-14",
      "given": "A user account with 4 consecutive failed login attempts",
      "when": "The user logs in successfully on the 5th attempt",
      "then": "The failed login counter resets to zero and the account is not locked"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-15",
      "given": "A partially-verified account with a $1,000 balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to withdraw $100",
      "then": "The withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating full verification is required"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Transfer amount exactly equals $5,000 daily limit for a verified account with $0 sent today", "expected_behavior": "Transaction succeeds — the limit is $5,000, not 'less than $5,000'. Boundary is inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "Transfer amount is exactly $1,000", "expected_behavior": "Notification is NOT sent — the spec says 'over $1,000', meaning the threshold is strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal." },
    { "scenario": "Recipient becomes sanctioned between transaction initiation and settlement", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous — spec does not define whether sanctions screening occurs only at initiation or also at settlement. Escalated below." },
    { "scenario": "Account locked, then user resets password via email flow", "expected_behavior": "Account remains locked — spec explicitly states only a support agent can unlock. Password reset does not bypass the lock." },
    { "scenario": "User sends exactly $500 from a partially-verified account across multiple transactions summing to $500", "expected_behavior": "All transactions succeed — the cumulative daily total equals but does not exceed the $500 limit." },
    { "scenario": "Unverified account receives $50,000 in a single transfer", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous — spec defines send limits by tier but does not specify inbound limits. The $1,000 notification should still fire. The receive itself should succeed per spec." },
    { "scenario": "5 failed logins spread across different calendar days (not consecutive in time)", "expected_behavior": "Account locks — the spec says '5 in a row' meaning consecutive attempts regardless of time span. The counter resets only on a successful login." },
    { "scenario": "A partially-verified account attempts to send to a sanctioned entity", "expected_behavior": "Blocked by sanctions screening (BR-5) before the daily limit check is even relevant. Sanctions check takes priority as a compliance gate." },
    { "scenario": "Transaction of exactly $1,001 is received by an unverified account", "expected_behavior": "Transfer succeeds (unverified can receive) AND a notification is sent (amount > $1,000)." },
    { "scenario": "Daily limit boundary at midnight — does the limit reset at midnight UTC, midnight local, or rolling 24h?", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous — spec says 'daily' but does not define the reset window. Escalated below." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Incorrect daily limit enforcement could lead to losses from fraud or regulatory fines. Settlement finality protects the platform from double-spend scenarios. Sanctions violations carry penalties of $250K–$1M+ per incident under OFAC.",
    "operational": "Account locking with manual-only unlock creates support volume. Each lock event requires a support agent interaction — staffing and SLA implications. Sanctions list must be kept current, requiring an operational feed.",
    "user": "Tiered verification creates friction for new users who want to send immediately. Unverified receive-only is a good onramp. Notifications for >$1,000 build trust. Account lockout after 5 attempts may frustrate legitimate users who mistype passwords.",
    "risk": "Primary risks are sanctions compliance failure (legal/criminal exposure), KYC bypass (regulatory), and settlement reversal bugs (financial integrity). The 5-attempt lockout is moderate protection — sophisticated attackers may use credential stuffing across accounts rather than brute-forcing one.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "End Users (Wallet Holders)", "impact": "Tiered access based on verification status affects usability. Lockout policy affects access. Notification policy affects trust and awareness." },
      { "group": "Compliance / Legal Team", "impact": "KYC gating and sanctions blocking are their primary controls. Failure here creates direct regulatory exposure for the company." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Manual unlock requirement drives ticket volume. Every locked account = a support interaction. Must have tooling to unlock accounts and verify caller identity." },
      { "group": "Finance / Treasury", "impact": "Settlement finality affects reconciliation. Daily limits affect liquidity planning and fraud exposure calculations." },
      { "group": "Product / Growth", "impact": "Verification friction affects conversion funnel. The receive-only unverified tier is a growth lever — users can see money waiting, motivating them to complete KYC." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is reasonably clear on most rules but leaves several operational details undefined: daily limit reset timing, sanctions re-screening at settlement, inbound transfer limits, notification channel/mechanism, and the exact definition of 'partially verified'. These gaps are non-trivial in a regulated financial product. No contradictions found, but the ambiguities reduce confidence below 75.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "KYC gating: unverified cannot send or withdraw (clearly stated, regulatory basis)",
      "Settlement finality at T+1 (clearly stated)",
      "Sanctions blocking requirement (clearly stated, regulatory basis)",
      "Account lockout after 5 consecutive failures with support-only unlock (clearly stated)",
      "Notification threshold at >$1,000 (clearly stated)"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Definition and criteria for 'partially-verified' status — spec mentions it exists but not what qualifies",
      "Daily limit reset timing — calendar day in which timezone, or rolling 24h?",
      "Whether sanctions screening applies only at initiation or also at settlement",
      "Whether there are any inbound transfer limits for any tier",
      "Notification channel (push, email, SMS, in-app) and retry policy on delivery failure",
      "Whether the $1,000 notification applies to both sender and receiver or just the account holder initiating"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Daily limits apply to outbound transfers only (sends and withdrawals), not receives",
      "The $1,000 notification threshold is strictly greater than, not >=, based on the word 'over'",
      "Daily limit of $5,000/$500 is per calendar day, not rolling 24 hours (assumed but ambiguous)",
      "Partially-verified accounts can send (up to $500/day) but cannot withdraw — inferred from the spec mentioning a send limit but the separate statement that only unverified cannot send",
      "Failed login counter is per-account and resets on successful login, not on a time window",
      "T+1 settlement means one business day, not one calendar day"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec introduces 'partially-verified accounts' with a $500 daily limit but does not define what constitutes partial verification vs. unverified vs. fully verified. Without this definition, the system cannot correctly assign tier limits.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance/Legal", "Product", "Engineering"],
      "options": ["Define partial verification as completing Step 1 (email/phone) but not Step 2 (ID document)", "Define partial verification as submitting KYC documents that are pending review", "Define explicit verification levels with named criteria"],
      "recommendation": "Define explicit verification tiers with named criteria and document in the domain model. This is critical for correct limit enforcement."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Daily transfer limit reset timing is undefined. 'Daily' could mean calendar day in UTC, calendar day in the user's local timezone, or a rolling 24-hour window. In a global product, this creates material differences in limit enforcement.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Finance/Treasury", "Engineering"],
      "options": ["Calendar day in UTC (simplest, consistent)", "Calendar day in user's local timezone (best UX, complex)", "Rolling 24-hour window (strictest, most complex)"],
      "recommendation": "Use calendar day in UTC for V1 — simplest to implement and audit. Document clearly for users."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec says partially-verified accounts have a $500 daily transfer limit, but also says unverified accounts cannot send. It is implied but not explicitly stated that partially-verified accounts CAN send (up to $500). Additionally, can partially-verified accounts withdraw?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Compliance/Legal", "Product"],
      "options": ["Partially-verified can send up to $500/day but cannot withdraw", "Partially-verified can both send and withdraw up to $500/day", "Clarify the full permission matrix for each tier"],
      "recommendation": "Produce an explicit permission matrix: [unverified: receive only], [partially-verified: send up to $500/day, receive, no withdraw], [verified: send up to $5,000/day, receive, withdraw]. Get PM sign-off."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Sanctions screening timing is not specified. The spec says block transfers to sanctioned entities, but does not address whether re-screening occurs between initiation and settlement (T+1). A recipient could be sanctioned during that window.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance/Legal", "Finance/Treasury"],
      "options": ["Screen at initiation only", "Screen at both initiation and settlement", "Screen at initiation and run batch re-screening on pending transactions daily"],
      "recommendation": "Screen at initiation AND run daily batch re-screening on all pending (unsettled) transactions. This is the standard approach in regulated payments."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The $1,000 notification rule does not specify: (a) who receives the notification — sender, receiver, or both? (b) what channel — push, email, SMS? (c) whether notification failure should block the transaction.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users", "Product", "Engineering"],
      "options": ["Notify sender only via preferred channel", "Notify both parties via all registered channels", "Notify sender via push + email, receiver via push only"],
      "recommendation": "At minimum, notify the account holder whose balance changed. Notification failure should NOT block the transaction — notifications are informational, not transactional gates."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
